theme: dam release scheduling against seeded rainfall
title: Two-Speed Reservoir: Dam Release Scheduling Against Seeded Rainfall
strategies: trivial: release 0 every day -- reproduces the evaluator's own weak baseline (~0.1); the level only ever rises until it spills

greedy: react to a FIXED target of 80% of capacity, releasing only the excess above it each day capped by Rmax -- 'keep the reservoir full for water security'; oblivious to the untagged slow-vs-fast structure of the inflow record, so any storm burst whose excess exceeds Rmax overtops it on the day it lands

strong: detect storm bursts via a rolling-median local baseline over the known inflow record, compute how much headroom (beyond what Rmax can absorb reactively) each upcoming burst needs within a lookahead window, and preemptively lower a dynamic release target in the days before -- scaled by the instance's own flood_coef/shortage_coef trade-off and floored above min_level so pre-draining never itself causes a shortage

headroom above strong: exact dynamic-programming / MPC-style optimization over the full release sequence (or a proper convex/LP relaxation of the water-balance constraints) to find the true minimum-penalty schedule, versus strong's single-pass lookahead heuristic which is not optimal
